segments.text: There was a certain man in Cæsarea called Cornelius, a centurion of the band called the Italian band
A devout man, and one that feared God with all his house, which gave much alms to the people, and prayed to God alway
He saw in a vision evidently about the ninth hour of the day
Cornelius
segments.speaker: An angel

segments.text: And when he looked on him, he was afraid
What is it, Lord?
segments.speaker: Cornelius

segments.text: Thy prayers and thine alms are come up for a memorial before God
segments.speaker: An angel

segments.text: Send men to Joppa, and call for one Simon, whose surname is Peter
segments.speaker: An angel

segments.text: He lodgeth with one Simon a tanner, whose house is by the sea side: he shall tell thee what thou oughtest to do
And when the angel which spake unto Cornelius was departed, he called two of his household servants, and a devout soldier of them that waited on him continually
And when he had declared all these things unto them, he sent them to Joppa
On the morrow, as they went on their journey, and drew nigh unto the city, Peter went up upon the housetop to pray about the sixth hour
And he became very hungry, and would have eaten: but while they made ready, he fell into a trance
And saw heaven opened, and a certain vessel descending unto him, as it had been a great sheet knit at the four corners, and let down to the earth
Wherein were all manner of fourfooted beasts of the earth, and wild beasts, and creeping things, and fowls of the air
Rise, Peter; kill, and eat
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Not so, Lord; for I have never eaten any thing that is common or unclean
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: What God hath cleansed, that call not thou common
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: This was done thrice: and the vessel was received up again into heaven
Now while Peter doubted in himself what this vision which he had seen should mean, behold, the men which were sent from Cornelius had made enquiry for Simon's house, and stood before the gate
And called, and asked whether Simon, which was surnamed Peter, were lodged there
Behold, three men seek thee
segments.speaker: The Spirit

segments.text: Arise therefore, and get thee down, and go with them, doubting nothing: for I have sent them
segments.speaker: The Spirit

segments.text: Then Peter went down to the men which were sent unto him from Cornelius
Behold, I am he whom ye seek: what is the cause wherefore ye are come?
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Cornelius the centurion, a just man, and one that feareth God, and of good report among all the nation of the Jews, was warned from God by an holy angel to send for thee into his house, and to hear words of thee
segments.speaker: Cornelius's Messengers

segments.text: Of a truth I perceive that God is no respecter of persons
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: While Peter yet spake these words, the Holy Ghost fell on all them which heard the word

heading: The Holy Spirit Comes to the Gentiles

segments.text: For they heard them speak with tongues, and magnify God
Can any man forbid water, that these should not be baptized, which have received the Holy Ghost as well as we?
segments.speaker: Peter
